name: Entertainment
description: Film, television, music, and pop-culture grab bag

questions.q: Which American reality competition show is famous for the phrase 'The tribe has spoken'?

source.notes: Delivered by host Jeff Probst after each tribal council vote.

questions.q: Jerry, George, Elaine, and Kramer are the four main characters of which '90s sitcom?

source.notes: Ran on NBC 1989–1998.

questions.q: In the long-running UK motoring show 'Top Gear', Jeremy Clarkson was famously joined by Hammond and which third host?

source.notes: The trio later launched 'The Grand Tour' on Amazon Prime.

questions.q: In Marvel comics, which superhero is bitten by a radioactive arachnid as teenager Peter Parker?

source.notes: Created by Stan Lee and Steve Ditko; first appeared in 1962.

questions.q: The sitcom 'The Big Bang Theory' is set in which sunny Californian city, home to Caltech?

source.notes: Sheldon and Leonard are Caltech physicists.

questions.q: Who played the heart-throb Dr. Doug Ross over the first five seasons of NBC's 'ER'?

source.notes: Clooney played Ross from 1994 to 1999.

questions.q: Kelsey Grammer played the sharp-witted psychiatrist Frasier Crane on which long-running show?

source.notes: Grammer played Frasier across 20 seasons of 'Cheers', 'Frasier', and the 2023 revival.

questions.q: Who performed the 1997 power ballad 'My Heart Will Go On' from the movie 'Titanic'?

source.notes: Written by James Horner and Will Jennings.

questions.q: What is the birth name of the Oscar-winning American actor Jamie Foxx?

source.notes: Full name Eric Marlon Bishop; won the Oscar for 'Ray' in 2005.

questions.q: Which actor played Batman in Tim Burton's 1989 film and its 1992 sequel 'Batman Returns'?

source.notes: Keaton reprised the role in 'The Flash' (2023).

questions.q: Which British director made his name with 'Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels' and 'Snatch'?

source.notes: Also directed 'Sherlock Holmes' (2009) and Disney's 'Aladdin' (2019).

questions.q: Scottish actor David Tennant became a fan favorite after playing which Doctor in 'Doctor Who'?

source.notes: Regenerated into Matt Smith in 2010; returned as the 14th Doctor in 2023.

questions.q: Agents Fox Mulder and Dana Scully are the FBI leads of which cult sci-fi TV series?

source.notes: Ran 1993–2002, revived 2016–2018. 'The truth is out there.'

questions.q: Which actor played gangster John Dillinger in the 2009 Michael Mann film 'Public Enemies'?

source.notes: Opposite Christian Bale as FBI agent Melvin Purvis.

questions.q: Which pop superstar played Eva Perón in the 1996 film adaptation of the musical 'Evita'?

source.notes: Won the Golden Globe for Best Actress in a Comedy or Musical.

questions.q: Complete the title of the Celine Dion 1994 hit: 'The Power of ___'.

source.notes: Originally a 1985 Jennifer Rush song; Dion's version was her first US #1.

questions.q: Brandon Lee, the son of Bruce Lee, tragically died from a gunshot accident on the set of which 1994 film?

source.notes: Died March 31, 1993 at age 28; film released posthumously in 1994.

questions.q: In the 'Jurassic World' films, which actor plays the Velociraptor trainer Owen Grady?

source.notes: Starred in 'Jurassic World', 'Fallen Kingdom', and 'Dominion'.

questions.q: In the classic 1959 comedy 'Some Like It Hot', Tony Curtis stars alongside which other iconic actor in drag?

source.notes: Marilyn Monroe also stars. Directed by Billy Wilder.

questions.q: Which US crime series was set in the sun-soaked tropical city of Miami, Florida?

source.notes: Miami Vice (1984–89), CSI: Miami (2002–12), and Dexter (2006–13) are all set in Miami.

questions.q: The 2003 indie hit 'Mr. Brightside' propelled which American alternative rock band to fame?

source.notes: From their debut album 'Hot Fuss'. Fronted by Brandon Flowers; Las Vegas-based.

questions.q: What was the full name of the bartender-owner at the Boston bar in the sitcom 'Cheers'?

source.notes: Played by Ted Danson; a former relief pitcher for the Boston Red Sox.
